forecaster_note: Wed 22 Jul — clean, light and all yours. A 4.5ft @ 10s ENE pulse from 67° sits right in your 30–80° sweet spot, 211 kJ, 100% consistency, and the wind barely shows: light WNW/W offshore under 8 kph all day, so there's no midday cut-off today — it stays surfable morning to arvo. Low tide 7:51am, high 2:43pm. Water a brisk 16.7°C, air ~15°C. Long Reef holds the most size and rates best. Heads up: a big dead-S groundswell lands for the weekend. — Surf Guru read on live Surfline data. ⚠️ SharkSmart detection feed was down this run — tap the live map before you paddle out.

wind.summary: Light WNW/W offshore all day, staying under 8 kph — a rare all-day clean window with no NE onshore to cut it short. Dawn to mid-arvo all good.

shark.summary: SharkSmart detection feed was unavailable this refresh (server error on the DPINSW / SLSNSW drone + drumline feeds) — Northern Beaches activity could not be confirmed. Tap through to the live map before paddling out.

days.label: Today
days.note: Rare clean all-day window — 4.5ft/10s ENE at 67° right in your sweet spot, 211 kJ, 100% consistency, under a light WNW offshore that never swings onshore. Long Reef holds the most size (chest to head) and rates best; Dee Why Point and Curl Curl are the fun, manageable calls. No rush this morning — it stays good.

dee-why.window: Light WNW offshore holds clean all day — no midday shutdown. Low 7:51am has the banks shallow and punchy first up; the push to the 2:43pm high gives the Point rights a longer wall. Mid-morning is prime.

tip.label: Frontside tip
tip.text: 67° is exactly the angle the Point wants — no dead-S wrap problem today. Sit wide on the rights, let the 10s period stack them, and with 211 kJ keep your rail engaged rather than waiting for it to throw for you.

long-reef.window: Best of the four again — the reef takes the full ENE energy and the offshore holds it open all day. Best on the push off the 7:51am low. Rights with real weight behind them.

tip.label: Frontside tip
tip.text: It'll feel a size up on Dee Why because it is. Commit early on the drop and use the offshore to hold your line through the first section.

curl-curl.window: Clean shifting peaks under the offshore all day. High wave count with 100% consistency — a good session to rack up waves and work on flow.

tip.label: Backside tip
tip.text: The straight ENE angle makes the lefts here run better than usual. Pick the peak with sand behind it and drive off the bottom early on your backhand rather than stalling for the pocket.

manly.window: Solid size at North Steyne under the offshore, a touch less groomed than the northern corners. Easy option if you want the shortest paddle before work.

tip.label: Backside tip
tip.text: Straight ENE lights up the whole stretch — walk until you find a bank peeling rather than settling for the first close-out in front of the car.

days.label: Tomorrow
days.note: Swell backs off — energy halves to ~105 kJ as the ENE pulse fades to 3ft @ 10s from 67°. Still clean under a W offshore (7 kph, gusts 14) through the morning, so a soft, tidy session rather than a powerful one. Long Reef keeps the most on offer.

dee-why.window: W offshore holds the morning — clean but noticeably weaker than today. Fun thigh-to-waist on the Point; extra volume under your feet won't hurt.

long-reef.window: Holds the most residual energy off the reef — the pick if you want size on a fading swell. Cleanest before 10am.

curl-curl.window: Tidy waist-to-chest peaks under the offshore. Easy, high-wave-count session — good for flow over power surfing.

manly.window: Clean and modest with the W offshore — the smallest, softest of the four. Fuller-volume board weather.

days.label: Weekend
days.note: A big S groundswell lands — energy leaps to 749 kJ Saturday and a huge 1348 kJ Sunday, the most raw power of the run. The catch is the angle: 6.6ft @ 11s from 184° Saturday and 7.9ft @ 11s from 180° Sunday is effectively dead south, which wraps poorly into Dee Why and is largely shadowed at Manly. Long Reef and Curl Curl are the S-exposed picks. Saturday morning carries a stiff W offshore (gusting 24); Sunday is lighter and cleaner (WSW 7–10 kph) but far bigger — respect it.

alerts: Dead-S swell (180–184°) — big energy on paper but it wraps poorly into Dee Why. Expect less size than the kJ suggests, but the Point can groom a clean wrapping right.
💨 Sat morning W gusting to 24 kph — clean but hard work.
dee-why.window: Both mornings run offshore. The Point is the play here — a dead-S swell wraps down and can hold a longer, cleaner right even as the beachies stay shadowed. Sunday has far more energy but 97% consistency means longer waits between sets.

alerts: 💨 Sat morning W gusting to 24 kph — chop on the faces despite being offshore.
Sunday jumps to 2ft overhead-plus — step-up board and treat it with respect.
long-reef.window: Most S-exposed and the size standout both days — overhead Saturday, well overhead-plus Sunday. Surfline marks it down for the gusty W, but it's where the swell actually shows. Saturday 8–11am is the cleaner window.

curl-curl.window: Takes the S swell far better than Dee Why — solid overhead peaks with the offshore holding them open. Saturday morning rates cleanest before Sunday's swell tops out. The best size-plus-shape balance of the weekend.

alerts: S swell is largely shadowed by the southern headland — smallest of the four despite the big energy figure.
manly.window: Clean but shadowed. Only worth it if the bigger spots are too much, or you want an easy paddle.
